entries.label: println! / print!
entries.note: Print to stdout/stderr; supports formatting specifiers

entries.label: format!
entries.note: Like println! but returns a String instead of printing

entries.label: vec!
entries.note: Create a Vec with literal values or repeated value

entries.label: assert macros
entries.note: Panics if assertion fails; optional message as last argument

entries.label: debug_assert!
entries.code:
debug_assert!(expensive_check());
debug_assert_eq!(a, b);
entries.note: Only checked in debug builds; compiled out in release

entries.label: todo! / unimplemented!
entries.note: Placeholder macros that panic; todo! for planned work, unimplemented! for deliberate gaps

entries.label: dbg!
entries.note: Debug printing that shows file, line, expression, and value; returns the value

entries.label: cfg! / env!
entries.note: cfg! checks configuration at compile time; env! reads environment variables at compile time

entries.label: Simple macro
entries.note: Define with macro_rules!; invoke with ! suffix

entries.label: With parameters
entries.note: $name:ident captures an identifier; other fragment types available

entries.label: Expression parameter
entries.note: $e:expr captures any Rust expression

entries.label: Multiple arms
entries.note: Macros can have multiple patterns, matched top to bottom

entries.label: Fragment specifiers
entries.note: Each fragment type constrains what can be matched

entries.label: Token trees
entries.note: tt matches any token; $($tt:tt)* matches everything

entries.label: Type parameter
entries.note: $t:ty captures type expressions

entries.label: Zero or more
entries.note: $(...),* repeats zero or more times with comma separator

entries.label: One or more
entries.note: $(...),+ repeats one or more times; recursive pattern for variadic

entries.label: Optional
entries.note: Multiple arms with different arities simulate optional parameters

entries.label: Nested repetition
entries.note: Nested $(...) for multi-dimensional patterns

entries.label: Common derives
entries.note: Auto-generate trait implementations; most common Rust metaprogramming

entries.label: Serde derive
entries.note: serde derives for JSON/TOML/etc serialization; most popular derive macro

entries.label: Available standard derives
entries.note: Standard library derive macros; all fields must implement the trait

entries.label: Custom derive (usage)
entries.note: Third-party crates provide custom derive macros via procedural macros

entries.label: Three kinds
entries.note: Procedural macros are Rust functions that transform token streams

entries.label: Derive macro skeleton
entries.note: Uses syn to parse and quote to generate code; must be in a separate crate

entries.label: Attribute macro skeleton
entries.note: Takes attribute args and the annotated item; returns modified code

entries.label: Function-like macro
entries.note: Called like sql!(...); can take arbitrary token input

entries.label: HashMap literal
entries.note: Common utility macro; $(,)? allows trailing comma

entries.label: Implement for multiple types
entries.note: Reduce boilerplate by implementing traits for multiple types at once

entries.label: Builder pattern macro
entries.note: Generate builder structs; real-world versions use proc macros

entries.label: stringify! and concat!
entries.note: stringify! converts tokens to string literal; concat! joins string literals at compile time

entries.label: include_str! / include_bytes!
entries.code:
const SQL: &str = include_str!("../schema.sql");
const ICON: &[u8] = include_bytes!("../icon.png");
entries.note: Embed file contents at compile time; path relative to source file

entries.label: Hygiene
entries.note: macro_rules! macros are partially hygienic; local vars don't leak

entries.label: Exporting macros
entries.note: #[macro_export] makes macro available to other crates

entries.label: Macro use in modules
entries.note: macro_rules! macros have textual scope; must be defined before use
